meta.title: Lancashire Combined County Authority
meta.legislation: The Lancashire Combined County Authority Regulations 2025

overview.summary: Lancashire's Combined County Authority brings together the three upper-tier councils to exercise devolved powers from central government. It manages transport, adult skills, housing, and economic development across Lancashire — powers previously held by Westminster, not transferred from local councils.
devolved_powers: Transport — integrated planning and delivery, bus services, active travel
Adult Education & Skills — Adult Skills Fund (£41M/yr), Local Skills Improvement Plans
Housing — strategic coordination of improvement, regeneration, new development
Economic Development — business investment, growth coordination, innovation
Environmental Policy — sustainability initiatives, low-carbon development

chair.name: Cllr Stephen Atkinson
chair.notes: Former Ribble Valley BC leader. Defected from Conservatives before May 2025 election. Also vice-chair of LGA and leader of Reform UK LGA group.

constituent_members.name: Cllr Phil Riley
constituent_members.role: Vice Chair, Transport Advisory Board Chair

key_officers.name: Denise Park
key_officers.role: Interim Chief Operating Officer / Head of Paid Service

advisory_boards.name: Transport Advisory Board
advisory_boards.focus: Integrated transport, bus services, active travel, Local Transport Plan

advisory_boards.name: Skills Advisory Board
advisory_boards.focus: Adult Skills Fund, workforce development, Get Lancashire Working

lancashire_business_board.role: Private sector advisory body providing business perspective

capital_projects.name: Silicon Sands Low Carbon Data Centre
Cosy Homes in Lancashire
capital_projects.notes: Energy efficiency home upgrade grant scheme

mayoral_question.summary: The dominant political tension. Reform UK opposes an elected mayor; Labour strongly favours one. Government can impose a mayor after 18 months of CCA existence (August 2026).

positions.detail: Initially sought binding referendum (£2.5M). Government refused. Now requesting detailed list of funding/powers a mayor would bring before any commitment. Requested deferral of mayoral election from 2026 to 2027.
Riley argues Lancashire will be 'nowhere' without a mayor, citing £1bn+ additional funding. Bradley accuses Reform of deliberately slowing the process.
Argues Lancashire is already 'losing companies' to Greater Manchester which has a mayor.

mayoral_question.government_position: Angela Rayner stated by May 2026 the whole North would have mayors. Legislation allows imposition after 18 months. Government has softened language to 'whether' rather than mandating.
mayoral_question.key_deadline_detail: 18 months from CCA establishment — government power to impose mayor triggers

lgr_relationship.summary: CCA and LGR are separate but linked. CCA exercises devolved powers from Westminster; LGR restructures local councils into unitaries. The CCA continues regardless of LGR outcome — any new unitary councils would become CCA constituents.
lgr_relationship.reform_position: Backs a two-authority solution for LGR. Opposes wholesale single-authority reorganisation.
lgr_relationship.timeline: Government consultation expected early 2026, preferred option summer 2026, shadow elections May 2027, new councils April 2028.

timeline.detail: Three upper-tier councils begin exploring combined authority options
Chancellor announces Lancashire devolution deal; deal signed
Labour Government formally approves the non-mayoral combined county authority proposal
The Lancashire Combined County Authority Regulations 2025 passes through Parliament
Shadow CCA meets at Preston County Hall
Lancashire Combined County Authority formally established
Constitution adopted. No member allowances for CCA duties agreed.
Second formal meeting of the authority
Reform wins 52 of 84 seats — takes control of Lancashire County Council. Phillippa Williamson loses seat.
Stephen Atkinson (Reform UK) elected CCA Chair, Phil Riley Vice Chair. Revenue budget and capital programme approved. £19.4M Growing Places Fund accepted. Get Lancashire Working plan agreed.
Reform UK asks government for £2.5M binding referendum on elected mayor
Government says non-binding referendum possible but won't fund binding one
Quarterly meeting
Quarterly meeting

timeline.event: Lancashire requests clarity on mayoral powers
timeline.detail: Atkinson asks government for detailed list of funding and powers a mayor would bring
Public consultation on Lancashire's first integrated Local Transport Plan
Quarterly meeting
2026-27 budget discussion
Target date for LCCA to become Local Transport Authority — £640M+ funding over 4 years
Upcoming
Upcoming — annual general meeting
Government power to impose elected mayor triggers 18 months after CCA establishment

achievements_first_year: Lancashire Growth Plan adopted — £20bn investment pipeline identified
£20M devolution capital funding received and allocated to 4 projects
Get Lancashire Working employment plan adopted
Innovation Hub at Samlesbury Enterprise Zone
ambitions_2026: £20M Local Innovation Partnership bid (defence, security, critical infrastructure)
Local Transport Authority status (£640M+ over 4 years)
UK Real Estate Investment and Infrastructure Forum (May 2026)
Final Lancashire Local Transport Plan submitted for approval

lancashire_cc.impact: Largest constituent. Growing Places Fund transferred (£19.4M). LCC leader chairs CCA. Transport powers moving to CCA Apr 2026.

blackpool.impact: Silicon Sands £2M + DWP offices £4M capital projects. Lynn Williams chairs Economy Advisory Board.

blackburn.impact: Innovation Quarter £6M capital project. Phil Riley is Vice Chair and chairs Transport Advisory Board.

burnley.impact: Benefits from county-wide transport improvements, adult skills funding, and Cosy Homes energy efficiency scheme.

hyndburn.impact: Benefits from transport integration, skills funding. Part of East Lancashire regeneration focus.

pendle.impact: Benefits from transport links, skills funding, housing improvement coordination.

rossendale.impact: Benefits from transport connectivity, skills funding, environmental initiatives.

lancaster.impact: Mark Davies (Lancaster CEO) is CCA Statutory Scrutiny Officer. Benefits from transport, skills, housing powers.

ribble_valley.impact: Atkinson's home council (former RV leader). Benefits from rural prosperity funding (£653K).

chorley.impact: Cllr Alistair Bradley represents district leaders on CCA. Benefits from transport, skills, innovation.

south_ribble.impact: Benefits from transport improvements, skills funding, Samlesbury Enterprise Zone proximity.

west_lancashire.impact: Benefits from transport links, skills funding, housing coordination.

wyre.impact: Cllr Michael Vincent represents district leaders on CCA. Benefits from transport, skills.

preston.impact: County Hall (CCA meeting venue) located in Preston. Benefits from city deal synergies, transport hub.

fylde.impact: Benefits from transport improvements, skills funding, Warton Enterprise Zone.
